title: Elvarg
wikitext:
{{External|rs|rsc=Dragon (Dragon slayer)}} {{Otheruses|the quest boss fought during [[Dragon Slayer I]]|the [[Nightmare Zone]] variant|Elvarg (Nightmare Zone)}} {{Infobox Monster |name = Elvarg |image = [[File:Elvarg.png|250px]] |release = [[23 September]] [[2001]] |update = Dragon quest online! |members = No |combat = 83 |size = 4 |examine = Roar! A dragon! |xpbonus1 = 0.0 |xpbonus2 = 2.5 |max hit = 8 ([[Slash]]), 70 ([[Dragonfire]]), 10 (with [[anti-dragon shield]]) |aggressive = Yes |poisonous = No |attack style = [[Slash]], [[Magic]], [[Dragonfire]] |attack speed = 4 |respawn = 50 |slayxp = 80 |cat = Green Dragons |hitpoints = 80 |att = 70 |str = 70 |def = 70 |mage = 70 |range = 1 |attbns = 0 |strbns = 0 |amagic = 0 |mbns = 0 |arange = 0 |rngbns = 0 |dstab = 20 |dslash = 40 |dcrush = 40 |dmagic = 30 |drange = 20 |elementalweaknesstype = Water |elementalweaknesspercent = 30 |immunepoison = No |immunevenom = No |id = 817 }} '''Elvarg''' is the [[dragon (race)|dragon]] that players have to kill in the [[Dragon Slayer I]] [[quest]]. She resides in the [[Crandor and Karamja Dungeon|dungeon]] below [[Crandor]]. She is a [[green dragon]], and is considerably stronger than the usual kind. Unlike other [[chromatic dragons]], Elvarg has a ''ranged'' [[dragonfire]] attack. Despite being a dragon, she is not vulnerable to [[dragonbane weapons]]. ==Location== {{LocTableHead|league=yes}} {{LocLine |name = Elvarg |location = [[Crandor and Karamja Dungeon]] |levels = 83 |members = No |mapID = 46 |plane = 0 |x:2852,y:9637 |mtype = pin |leagueRegion = Karamja }} {{LocTableBottom}} ==History== During the [[Dragonkin Conflicts]], the [[Dragonkin]] needed to defend themselves from humans, who refused to distinguish peaceful and aggressive dragonkin.{{CiteNPC|npc=Zorgoth|loc=Dragon Slayer II|quote=You attacked us, tried to destroy us. You took away our homes, our people. Were we expected to stand by and watch? [Bob the Cat: We were defending ourselves. You threatened to destroy us all.] Not all of us. Only a few gave in to the anger of the curse. Only a few waged war on you. But you were not willing to distinguish. And now, neither am I.}} [[Zorgoth]] and other unnamed Dragonkin worked on creating [[dragon (race)|dragons]] with increased aggression.{{CiteNPC|npc=Zorgoth|loc=Dragon Slayer II|quote=After your failed attempt on my life, I realised that we were starting to lose the war against your kind. We needed to turn the tide. We needed a new weapon.}} The first of these experiments took place underneath [[Crandor]], where they successfully created a green dragon with heightened aggression: Elvarg.{{CiteText|text=Old notes (Dragon Slayer II)|quote=This trial was different though, the subject did not reject the spores as previous ones did. Alas, the results were unsatisfactory. The subject displayed heightened aggression, as desired. However, its strength is unchanged and its mental capacity is limited.}}{{CiteNPC|npc=Zorgoth|loc=Dragon Slayer II|quote=The creature you call Elvarg was the first. An attempt to create a being which would crush humanity once and for all. She was a failure. Too weak. Pathetic.}} Eventually, she was abandoned by her creators, making her home in the underground system underneath the island. [[File:Elvarg adventurer.png|thumb|Remains of the original adventurer who woke Elvarg|none]] Elvarg went into a deep slumber, until the year 139 of the [[Fifth Age]] when a Crandorian adventurer discovered the cave system and explored the area, eventually stumbling into Elvarg's chamber.{{CiteNPC|npc=Guildmaster|loc=Dragon Slayer I|quote=Thirty years ago, Crandor was a thriving community with a great tradition of mages and adventurers. Many Crandorians even earned the right to be part of the Champions' Guild! One of their adventurers went too far, however. He descended into the volcano in the centre of Crandor and woke the dragon Elvarg.}} The adventurer roused her from her sleep, and she torched them in retaliation before leaving the caverns and torching Crandor completely, its inhabitants not having any sort of dragonfire protection to fight back. Crandorian survivors made a small camp near [[Rimmington]], but Elvarg pursued them and killed most of the survivors, with only three mages able to escape; [[Melzar]], [[Lozar]] and [[Thalzar]], each of whom split a map to the island and went their separate ways.{{CiteNPC|npc=Guildmaster|loc=Dragon Slayer I|quote=Some refugees managed to escape in fishing boats. They landed on the coast, north of Rimmington, and set up camp but the dragon followed them and burned the camp to the ground. Out of all the people of Crandor there were only three survivors: a trio of wizards who used magic to escape. Their names were Thalzar, Lozar and Melzar.}} The human kingdoms of [[Misthalin]] were on high alert, worried she might devastate the continent, but fortunately she returned to her chamber on the island.{{CiteNPC|npc=Duke Horacio|loc=Dragon Slayer I|quote=I remember my father being very scared. He posted lookouts on the roof to warn if the dragon was approaching. All the city rulers were worried that Elvarg would devastate the whole continent.}} During the year 169 of the Fifth Age, an adventurer who entered the [[Champions' Guild]] looked for a quest; the [[Guildmaster]] told them to speak to [[Oziach]], who in turn told them to slay Elvarg. The adventurer acquired equipment, including an [[anti-dragon shield]] from [[Duke Horacio]], and the map from the three mages (two of which had died, while the other went insane), a ship, and a captain who was not intimidated by her reputation. They soon came upon the chamber where Elvarg was, and successfully slew her after a grueling battle, ripping her head off as proof of her demise.[[File:Dragon Slayer - Elvarg slain.png|thumb|250px|The player beheads Elvarg|none]] ==Dragonfire== The [[dragonfire]] max damage only depends on your [[Dragonfire#Protection|dragonfire protection]] (prayer, potion, and/or shield). The [[super antifire potion]] (including the [[Extended super antifire|extended]] and [[Super antifire mix|mix]] versions) by itself is enough to prevent all damage (even without an [[anti-dragon shield]]) and stat drain, except for [[Prayer points]] which will still be drained every attack. Every dragonfire attack will deduct 10% of the Player's remaining [[Prayer points]], rounded down. {{:Dragonfire/Elvarg}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 25 June 2025 |update = Summer Sweep Up: Combat |change = Elvarg now has a 30% [[elemental weakness]] to [[water spells]]. }} {{Subject changes |date = 29 August 2006 |update = Demons, Dragons and Giants |change = The NPC was graphically updated. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ===Gallery (historical)=== {|style="text-align: center" cellpadding="15" |[[File:Elvarg (historical).png|center|300x150px]] |[[File:Elvarg.png|center|300x150px]] |- |29 March 2004 –
29 August 2006 |29 August 2006 –
present |} ==Gallery== Elvarg (Trading Card).png|The Elvarg [[Steam Trading Cards|Steam Trading Card]]. ==Trivia== *Elvarg was confirmed to be female in the [[Transcript:You enjoy Postbag from the Hedge.|16th]] [[Postbag from the Hedge]], by such quotes as ''"She wouldn't 'a had second thoughts about roasting yer with her breath..."'' and ''"That's what she done to Crandor, after all..."'' ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Succession|predecessor=[[Greater demon]]|title=[[Strongest monster|Strongest monster in ''RuneScape'']]|successor=[[Black dragon]]}} {{Dragon}} {{Dragon Slayer I}} {{Crandor and Karamja Dungeon}} [[Category:Bosses]] [[Category:Quest monsters]]
